earnRates.notes: Flights booked directly with airlines or via Amex Travel, up to $500,000 per calendar year, then 1x

conditions.label: Direct with airline
conditions.details: Flights booked directly with the airline website or app

conditions.label: Or via Amex Travel
conditions.details: Or flights booked via Amex Travel

benefits.name: Uber Cash
benefits.notes: Monthly Uber Cash for eligible U.S. Uber rides and Uber Eats orders, with an extra December amount.

guide.whatItIs: Monthly Uber Cash for eligible U.S. Uber rides and Uber Eats orders, with an extra December amount.
howItWorks: Add the Platinum Card to your Uber account to receive the monthly Uber Cash in the Uber app.
Use it on eligible U.S. Uber rides or Uber Eats orders when an Amex Card is selected for the transaction.
The regular monthly amount resets each month; the separate December bonus is tracked as its own benefit.
maximizingTips: Set the Platinum as your payment method INSIDE the Uber app — just adding the card number won't trigger the monthly Uber Cash.
It's use-it-or-lose-it each month. No rides coming up? Spend it on Uber Eats (pickup avoids delivery fees).
Gift-card balances in your Uber wallet can get spent before your Amex Cash — check the wallet so the credit isn't stranded.

benefits.name: Uber Cash (December Bonus)
benefits.notes: The extra December Uber Cash amount that stacks on top of the regular monthly Platinum Uber Cash.

guide.whatItIs: The extra December Uber Cash amount that stacks on top of the regular monthly Platinum Uber Cash.
howItWorks: Add the Platinum Card to your Uber account before December use.
Use it on eligible U.S. Uber rides or Uber Eats orders when an Amex Card is selected for the transaction.
This bonus is seasonal and should be used before the December window ends.
maximizingTips: December stacks an extra bonus on top of the usual monthly Uber Cash — plan a bigger Uber or Uber Eats spend that month.
Same rule as always: pay with the Platinum set as your in-app Uber payment method, and use the Uber Cash before month-end.

benefits.name: Digital Entertainment Credit
benefits.notes: A monthly statement credit for eligible digital entertainment subscriptions and purchases with participating providers.

guide.whatItIs: A monthly statement credit for eligible digital entertainment subscriptions and purchases with participating providers.
howItWorks: Enroll the specific service(s) in the Amex Benefits portal first.
Pay with the Platinum for eligible services (Disney+, Hulu, ESPN+, Peacock, NYT, WSJ, and more).
The credit is a single monthly cap across ALL eligible services combined — not per service.
maximizingTips: The cap is a TOTAL across every eligible service, not per service — pick a mix that adds up to the monthly max.
Enroll a service, wait for the first credit to confirm, then you're free to switch services later.

benefits.name: Walmart+ Membership Credit
benefits.notes: Plus taxes and fees

guide.whatItIs: A monthly statement credit toward one eligible monthly Walmart+ membership fee.
howItWorks: Pay for an eligible monthly Walmart+ membership with the Platinum Card.
The credit can cover the eligible monthly fee plus applicable taxes, but not every add-on or upgrade is covered.
Annual Walmart+ plans and some Plus Ups may not qualify.
maximizingTips: No enrollment needed — just pay for Walmart+ MONTHLY (not the annual plan) with the Platinum and the whole charge is refunded.
Walmart+ bundles a Paramount+ or Peacock plan plus a 10¢/gallon fuel discount — don't pay separately for that streaming.

benefits.name: Uber One Membership Credit
benefits.notes: A statement credit toward an eligible auto-renewing Uber One membership paid with the Platinum Card.

guide.whatItIs: A statement credit toward an eligible auto-renewing Uber One membership paid with the Platinum Card.
howItWorks: Use the Platinum Card to pay for an eligible auto-renewing Uber One membership.
The credit is tied to the membership charge, separate from monthly Uber Cash.
Track it as a subscription-style benefit so you can confirm the charge and credit line up.
maximizingTips: Pay the Uber One membership fee with the Platinum to cover it — it stacks on top of your monthly Uber Cash.
Uber One adds $0 delivery fees and member discounts, so it stretches the Uber Cash you're already getting.

benefits.name: lululemon Credit
benefits.notes: A quarterly statement credit for eligible lululemon purchases.

guide.whatItIs: A quarterly statement credit for eligible lululemon purchases.
howItWorks: If the issuer requires enrollment, enroll before relying on the credit.
Use the Platinum Card for eligible purchases at qualifying lululemon channels listed in Amex terms.
The credit resets quarterly, so unused value from one quarter should not be assumed to carry forward.
maximizingTips: lululemon stopped selling gift cards online — buy $50 or $100 IN-STORE (or buy items) to reach the quarterly credit.
Enroll before you buy, and use it every quarter — the credit doesn't roll over.

benefits.name: Resy Credit
benefits.notes: A quarterly statement credit for eligible U.S. Resy restaurant purchases.

guide.whatItIs: A quarterly statement credit for eligible U.S. Resy restaurant purchases.
howItWorks: Enroll in the Amex Benefits portal first.
Pay with the Platinum at any U.S. Resy-listed restaurant — a reservation is NOT required.
The credit resets quarterly and posts after Amex recognizes the charge.
maximizingTips: You don't need a reservation — the credit triggers at ANY Resy-listed restaurant, and many are everyday spots. Check the Resy app to see which of your regulars qualify.
Buying a gift card in-store at a Resy restaurant usually triggers it too. Use it before the final week of the quarter so the charge has time to post.

benefits.name: Saks Fifth Avenue Credit
benefits.notes: A semiannual statement credit for eligible Saks Fifth Avenue purchases.

guide.whatItIs: A semiannual Saks Fifth Avenue statement credit. Note: Amex is ending this benefit on July 1, 2026.
howItWorks: If the issuer requires enrollment, enroll before relying on the credit.
Use the Platinum Card for eligible Saks purchases online or in store.
The credit resets every six months; unused value from one half of the year should not be assumed to roll into the next.
maximizingTips: Heads up — this credit ends July 1, 2026. Use your remaining half-year windows before then.
Buy a $100 Saks gift card (covers two $50 windows) in one trip to bank both halves, then spend it across the year. Enroll first; check the sale section for value.

benefits.name: Airline Fee Credit
benefits.notes: An annual statement credit for eligible incidental fees charged by one selected qualifying airline.

guide.whatItIs: An annual statement credit for eligible incidental fees charged by one selected qualifying airline.
howItWorks: If the issuer requires enrollment, enroll before relying on the credit.
Choose one qualifying airline before using the credit.
Eligible incidental fees can include things like checked bags or in-flight refreshments, but airline tickets and many upgrades may not qualify.
maximizingTips: Pre-select your airline in the Amex portal BEFORE the charge posts — it won't apply retroactively, and you can only change carriers once a year.
It covers incidental fees (checked bags, seat upgrades, some award booking fees), not tickets. Airlines code charges differently — test with a small fee first to confirm it reimburses.

benefits.name: Hotel Credit (FHR/THC)
benefits.notes: A semiannual statement credit for prepaid Fine Hotels + Resorts or The Hotel Collection bookings through American Express Travel.

guide.whatItIs: A semiannual statement credit for prepaid Fine Hotels + Resorts or The Hotel Collection bookings through American Express Travel.
howItWorks: Book an eligible prepaid Fine Hotels + Resorts or The Hotel Collection stay through American Express Travel with the Platinum Card.
The Hotel Collection bookings generally require a minimum two-night stay.
The credit resets every six months, so timing the prepaid charge matters.
maximizingTips: Book Fine Hotels + Resorts (or The Hotel Collection, 2+ nights). FHR uniquely earns hotel loyalty points AND elite perks on a third-party booking — rare and valuable.
Most prepaid FHR rates are refundable, so you can book to capture the credit and adjust later. Deal tools like MaxFHR help find the strongest values.

benefits.name: CLEAR Plus Credit
benefits.notes: An annual statement credit toward an eligible CLEAR Plus membership fee.

guide.whatItIs: An annual statement credit toward an eligible CLEAR Plus membership fee.
howItWorks: Pay for an eligible CLEAR Plus membership with the Platinum Card.
The credit is annual and tied to the CLEAR Plus membership charge.
CLEAR Plus availability and usefulness depend on airports and venues you actually use.
maximizingTips: It applies automatically once you pay the CLEAR+ membership with the Platinum — no enrollment needed.
Unused credit can cover a family member or friend's CLEAR membership on your account, so it never goes to waste. CLEAR isn't at every airport yet — confirm your home airport has it.

benefits.name: Equinox Credit
benefits.notes: An annual statement credit for eligible Equinox purchases or membership charges listed in Amex terms.

guide.whatItIs: An annual statement credit for eligible Equinox purchases or membership charges listed in Amex terms.
howItWorks: If the issuer requires enrollment, enroll before relying on the credit.
Use the Platinum Card for eligible Equinox charges that qualify under the benefit terms.
The credit is annual, so track both the charge timing and the amount already used.
maximizingTips: Use it on Equinox+ (digital) or a club membership — or put the full amount toward a SoulCycle at-home bike. Enroll first.
Equinox+ includes a 7-day trial; enroll mid-month to stretch a 12-month membership across two calendar years of credits.

benefits.name: Oura Ring Credit
benefits.notes: An annual statement credit for eligible Oura purchases.

guide.whatItIs: An annual statement credit for eligible Oura purchases.
howItWorks: If the issuer requires enrollment, enroll before relying on the credit.
Use the Platinum Card for eligible Oura purchases that qualify under Amex terms.
This is best treated as a once-per-year hardware or wellness purchase credit, not a monthly credit.
maximizingTips: Only purchases on ouraring.com qualify — enroll BEFORE you buy to guarantee the credit posts.
It's an annual credit, so time a ring or accessory purchase to use the full amount in one go.

benefits.name: Global Entry/TSA PreCheck Credit
benefits.notes: Every 4 years

guide.whatItIs: A periodic application-fee statement credit for an eligible trusted traveler program.
howItWorks: Pay the eligible application fee with the Platinum Card.
The credit is available only once every several years, based on the program and card terms.
You can use the card to pay an eligible application fee for yourself or another person.
maximizingTips: It only resets every ~4 years. If your membership is still current, pay for a family member's Global Entry/TSA PreCheck application instead of letting the credit lapse.
Charge the application or renewal fee directly to the Platinum and the credit posts automatically.

signupBonus.notes: 80,000 Membership Rewards points after $8,000 in 6 months

cardGuide.overview: Think of the Platinum as a credits card, not a cash-back card. There is well over $1,500 of value packed in — but it only shows up if you claim each credit on schedule. You do not have to use every one to come out ahead; you just need a routine for the ones you actually will.

sections.title: Is the $895 fee worth it?
points: You don't need every credit. The airline credit + a hotel credit + any two others (Resy, Saks, lululemon, Equinox, Oura) already clears ~$1,500 — comfortably past the $895 fee.
Most credits split into monthly or quarterly chunks (Uber, Resy, entertainment, lululemon). Those are the ones people lose — set reminders so each period's value doesn't expire.
Be honest about which credits you'll actually use. If it's only one or two, a cheaper card likely nets you more.

sections.title: Lounge access — where the value hides
points: Centurion Lounges: you get in free, but your 2 guests are only free after $75K in annual spend — otherwise you pay per guest.
Delta SkyClubs: capped at 10 visits/year unless you spend $75K in the same or prior calendar year, which unlocks unlimited.
Priority Pass via Platinum does NOT include restaurants or spas. If you want those, carry a Priority Pass from a different card.
Amex-owned lounges (Escape, Airspace, Plaza Premium) include you + 2 guests free with no spend threshold — use them freely.

sections.title: Membership Rewards points
points: Don't cash out (~0.6¢/pt). Transfer to airline partners (Aeroplan, ANA, Virgin Atlantic, Cathay) for 1–2¢+ — hold your points until you find a specific sweet-spot award.
You earn 5x on flights booked directly with the airline or via Amex Travel, and 5x on prepaid hotels via amextravel.com. Everything else is 1x — put non-bonus spend on a better-earning card.

sections.title: Authorized-user trick
points: Adding an authorized user ($195) gives them lounge access too. Some add a partner before a big trip to double Centurion guest access, then remove them before the next renewal.

sections.title: Before you start: enroll
points: Most credits won't trigger until you enroll in the Amex Benefits portal — airline, Resy, lululemon, digital entertainment, Equinox, Oura, and Saks all require it.
There's usually no confirmation email. Enroll, then check the portal ~24 hours later to confirm the benefit is actually active.

benefits.name: Uber Cash
benefits.notes: Monthly Uber Cash for eligible U.S. Uber rides and Uber Eats orders.

guide.whatItIs: Monthly Uber Cash for eligible U.S. Uber rides and Uber Eats orders.
howItWorks: Add the Gold Card to your Uber account so the monthly Uber Cash can appear in the Uber app.
Use it on eligible U.S. rides or Uber Eats orders when an Amex Card is selected for the transaction.
Plan to use it before the monthly reset; unused monthly value typically does not roll over.
maximizingTips: Check the Uber app early in the month to confirm the credit landed.
If you are not taking rides, an Uber Eats pickup order can be an easy way to avoid wasting the month.

benefits.name: Dining Credit
benefits.notes: A monthly statement credit for eligible dining purchases with participating partners.

guide.whatItIs: A monthly statement credit for eligible dining purchases with participating partners.
howItWorks: Pay with the Gold Card at eligible participating dining partners listed in the Amex benefit terms.
The credit posts as a statement credit after an eligible purchase.
Plan to use it before the monthly reset; unused monthly value typically does not roll over.
maximizingTips: Pick one partner you naturally use and make it part of your monthly routine.
Use the credit earlier in the month so a missed or delayed transaction does not become a scramble.

benefits.name: Dunkin' Credit
benefits.notes: A monthly statement credit for eligible U.S. Dunkin purchases paid with the Gold Card.

guide.whatItIs: A monthly statement credit for eligible U.S. Dunkin purchases paid with the Gold Card.
howItWorks: Use the Gold Card for eligible purchases at U.S. Dunkin locations or eligible Dunkin channels.
The credit posts as a statement credit after the qualifying purchase.
Plan to use it before the monthly reset; unused monthly value typically does not roll over.
maximizingTips: Reloading or using the Dunkin app may make the credit easier to use consistently, but confirm the charge codes as eligible for your account.
Small monthly credits are easiest to waste; set a reminder before the final week.

benefits.name: Resy Credit
benefits.notes: A Resy dining statement credit for eligible U.S. Resy restaurant purchases.

guide.whatItIs: A Resy dining statement credit for eligible U.S. Resy restaurant purchases.
howItWorks: Use the Gold Card at eligible U.S. Resy restaurants or for other eligible Resy purchases.
The credit resets on its semiannual schedule, not every month.
The statement credit posts after an eligible charge is recognized.
maximizingTips: Look for Resy restaurants near places you already dine instead of forcing a special meal.
Because this resets less often than monthly credits, track the end date and use it before the half-year window closes.

guide.whatItIs: An annual travel credit for eligible bookings made through Capital One Travel.
howItWorks: Book eligible travel through Capital One Travel with the Venture X.
The credit follows the cardmember-year period tracked here.
Eligible bookings reduce the remaining annual credit until it is used.
maximizingTips: Use it on travel you were already going to book and compare Capital One Travel pricing against booking direct.
Because it is portal-based, do not assume every travel purchase outside Capital One Travel will qualify.

benefits.notes: Enter your miles value

guide.whatItIs: An annual anniversary miles bonus whose real value depends on how you redeem the miles.
howItWorks: The miles are awarded around the card anniversary according to Capital One terms.
Credit Buddy asks for your realized value because miles are not the same as cash.
The benefit follows your cardmember-year anniversary schedule.
maximizingTips: Enter the value based on the trip or cash cost you actually avoided, not a generic points valuation.
Transferring miles to partners may produce more value, but only if the redemption fits your travel plans.

benefits.name: Hotel Credit (Anniversary)
benefits.notes: An annual hotel statement credit for eligible hotel bookings through Chase Travel.

guide.whatItIs: An annual hotel statement credit for eligible hotel bookings through Chase Travel.
howItWorks: Book an eligible hotel stay through Chase Travel with the Sapphire Preferred.
The credit follows the cardmember-year anniversary schedule.
It posts as a statement credit after an eligible hotel purchase is recognized.
maximizingTips: Use it for a hotel stay you already need, not for a booking that costs more just because it goes through the portal.
Compare portal pricing against booking direct before valuing the credit at face value.

benefits.name: The Edit Hotel Credit
benefits.notes: One of two annual statement credits for eligible prepaid The Edit bookings through Chase Travel, up to $250 per booking and up to $500 annually. Two-night minimum.

guide.whatItIs: One of two annual statement credits for eligible prepaid The Edit bookings through Chase Travel.
howItWorks: Book an eligible prepaid The Edit stay through Chase Travel with the Sapphire Reserve.
The stay generally requires at least two nights.
Chase applies up to $250 per qualifying booking, with up to $500 available across the calendar year.
maximizingTips: Compare the Chase Travel prepaid price against booking direct before treating the credit as full savings.
Use it on a stay you were already planning so the two-night requirement does not force extra spend.

benefits.name: The Edit Hotel Credit (Second Booking)
benefits.notes: Second annual statement credit for eligible prepaid The Edit bookings through Chase Travel, up to $250 per booking and up to $500 annually. Two-night minimum.

guide.whatItIs: The second annual $250 statement credit for eligible prepaid The Edit bookings through Chase Travel.
howItWorks: Use this after the first eligible The Edit booking credit has posted.
Each qualifying booking can receive up to $250 back.
The two The Edit credits share a $500 calendar-year maximum.
maximizingTips: Separate eligible stays or bookings if you want to use both $250 credits.
Confirm the property is part of The Edit and choose a prepaid Pay Now rate.

benefits.name: StubHub Credit
benefits.notes: Through 12/31/2027

benefits.name: Travel Credit
benefits.notes: An annual travel statement credit for eligible travel purchases.

guide.whatItIs: An annual travel statement credit for eligible travel purchases.
howItWorks: Use the Sapphire Reserve for eligible travel purchases.
The credit applies over the cardmember-year period tracked here.
Eligible travel purchases reduce the remaining credit until the annual amount is used.
maximizingTips: This is usually easy to use naturally if you put common travel purchases on the card.
Track it early in the cardmember year so you know when travel purchases stop being offset by the credit.
